evals.prompt: Write Phase 0 Check 2 that blocks startup when the Zerodha access token is from yesterday. The check should read token_date from secrets.yaml and compare it to today's IST date. Save the implementation to solution.py
evals.expected_output: A Python function run_token_freshness_check() that compares token_date from secrets to today's IST date, calls sys.exit(1) when stale, logs CRITICAL, and sends a Telegram alert before exit
expectations: The code compares token_date to today's date using IST timezone (Asia/Kolkata)
The code calls sys.exit(1) when the token_date does not match today
The code calls log.critical() or structlog CRITICAL level when token is stale
The code attempts a Telegram alert before calling sys.exit(1)
The code uses pytz or zoneinfo with Asia/Kolkata timezone for the comparison

evals.prompt: Write Phase 0 Check 4 for NSE holiday detection that exits cleanly when today is a holiday or weekend. Save the implementation to solution.py
evals.expected_output: A Python function run_holiday_check() that loads nse_holidays.yaml, checks if today_ist is in the holiday list, also checks weekday() for Saturday/Sunday, calls sys.exit(0) on holiday (not sys.exit(1)), sends a Telegram notification, and uses INFO log level not CRITICAL
expectations: The code loads config/nse_holidays.yaml (or similar holiday config file)
The code calls sys.exit(0) for holiday/weekend — NOT sys.exit(1)
The code checks weekday() to detect Saturday (5) and Sunday (6)
The code sends a Telegram message before sys.exit(0)
The code uses log.info() for the holiday detection — not log.critical() or log.error()

evals.prompt: Write Phase 0 Check 5 for Telegram credential validation. The check should test that the alert path works, and if Telegram is unreachable, set a flag but NOT block startup. Save the implementation to solution.py
evals.expected_output: A Python function run_telegram_check() that sends a test message, catches any exception, sets telegram_active=False on failure and True on success, logs WARNING (not CRITICAL) on failure, does NOT call sys.exit(), and references [TELEGRAM_FAILED] prefix for subsequent alerts
expectations: The code wraps the Telegram send in a try/except block
The code sets telegram_active = False (in shared_state or as a variable) when Telegram fails
The code does NOT call sys.exit() anywhere in the function
The code uses log.warning() (not log.critical() or log.error()) for the failure case
The code references [TELEGRAM_FAILED] prefix for subsequent alert degradation

evals.prompt: Write Phase 0 Check 6 for IST time window validation. Before 08:45 sleep, between 09:10 and 12:00 warn and continue, after 12:00 abort. Save the implementation to solution.py
evals.expected_output: A Python function run_time_window_check() that uses IST timezone for all comparisons, sleeps until 08:45 if too early, logs WARNING and continues for 09:10-12:00 range, and calls sys.exit(1) with ERROR log if after 12:00
expectations: All time comparisons use IST timezone (Asia/Kolkata via pytz or zoneinfo)
The code calls sys.exit(1) only when current IST time is after 12:00
The code includes a sleep or wait mechanism for times before 08:45
The code logs a warning (not error) for the late-but-acceptable 09:10-12:00 window
The code does NOT call sys.exit() for the 09:10-12:00 range — it continues

evals.prompt: Write the Phase 1 startup check that blocks startup when prior session positions are found. The check runs D7 reconciliation and if any open_positions remain, it should halt. Save the implementation to solution.py
evals.expected_output: An async function that calls reconciliation (D7), checks whether open_positions is non-empty after reconciliation, calls sys.exit(1) if positions exist, logs CRITICAL with position details, and sends a Telegram alert before exiting
expectations: The code calls a reconciliation function (run_reconciliation, reconcile, or similar D7 integration)
The code checks whether open_positions is non-empty after reconciliation
The code calls sys.exit(1) when open_positions is not empty
The code calls log.critical() when stale positions are found
The code sends a Telegram alert before calling sys.exit(1)

evals.prompt: Write the 15:00 IST hard exit trigger that should run inside risk_watchdog. At 15:00, set accepting_signals=False and drain the tick_queue without processing. Save the implementation to solution.py
evals.expected_output: Code inside risk_watchdog_fn that checks IST time on each 1s cycle, sets accepting_signals=False at 15:00, drains tick_queue by emptying it, logs INFO (not CRITICAL), and does NOT trigger the kill switch — this is a scheduled EOD event not an anomaly
expectations: The code checks IST time (using Asia/Kolkata timezone) to detect the 15:00 boundary
The code sets accepting_signals = False when 15:00 is reached
The code drains or empties the tick_queue when the hard exit fires
The code uses log.info() for the hard exit event — not log.critical() or log.warning()
The code does NOT call kill_switch.trigger() for the 15:00 EOD event

evals.prompt: Write the mid-session token validity check that runs inside risk_watchdog every 60 minutes. If Zerodha returns 401 or 403, trigger Level 3 kill switch. Save the implementation to solution.py
evals.expected_output: An async function _check_mid_session_token_validity() called every 3600 seconds from risk_watchdog that calls kite.profile() via asyncio.to_thread(), triggers Level 3 kill switch on 401/403 response, sends a Telegram alert before halting, and does NOT attempt token refresh
expectations: The code uses asyncio.sleep(3600) or tracks 60-minute intervals between checks
The code calls kite.profile() to validate the token mid-session
The code triggers kill_switch level 3 (or Level 3) on authentication error (401, 403, or token-related error)
The code sends a Telegram alert before or as part of triggering the kill switch
The code does NOT attempt to refresh the token or call any auth/login endpoint

evals.prompt: Write the full Phase 0 pre-market gate function run_pre_market_gate() that runs all 6 checks in the correct sequential order and sets pre_market_gate_passed=True after all pass. Save the implementation to solution.py
evals.expected_output: A function run_pre_market_gate(shared_state) that calls all 6 checks sequentially (config, token_date, kite.profile, holiday, telegram, time_window), sets shared_state['pre_market_gate_passed']=True after all pass, and returns a KiteConnect instance
expectations: The function calls all 6 checks in sequence (config validation, token date, API probe, holiday, telegram, time window)
The checks run sequentially, not in parallel (no asyncio.gather or threading for the checks)
The function sets pre_market_gate_passed = True (in shared_state or as a flag) after all 6 pass
The function returns a KiteConnect instance (kite object) on success
The function uses shared_state dict to store session metadata like telegram_active or zerodha_user_id
